Topical steroids are indeed most effective in eczematous dermatitis—not just because it’s common, but because these conditions involve robust T-cell mediated inflammation directly responsive to steroid-induced immunosuppression. This makes them uniquely well-suited compared to bullous herpes or zoster cases, where viral replication dominates over inflammation. Therefore, despite some overlap in treatment approaches, the primary therapeutic niche of topical steroids remains eczematous dermatitis. Final answer: **B**
